channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Benchmark Leads $50M Series B for AI Agent Builder Gumloop
item.description: Benchmark led a $50 million Series B investment in AI agent builder Gumloop, the first major deal for new General Partner Everett Randle. The funding highlights investor belief in Gumloop's AI agents designed to help non-technical employees automate complex tasks. This capital will speed Gumloop's growth in the fast-expanding enterprise AI market, emphasizing its simple use and ability to work with various AI models.

item.title: Blinkit Lands ₹450 Cr for Expansion Amid Fierce Competition
item.description: Zomato's quick commerce arm, Blinkit, has received a ₹450 crore investment from its parent company. This funding aims to bolster Blinkit's growth and operations in India's highly competitive market, where rivals like Zepto and Swiggy Instamart are also expanding aggressively. Blinkit recently achieved adjusted EBITDA profitability in Q3 FY26, a key step following significant past losses, and is now central to Zomato's expansion plans.

item.title: India Gas Volumes Plunge as Conflict, Curbs Hit IGX
item.description: India's top gas platform IGX sees lower trades and marketer hesitation due to volatile global gas prices and new supply rules. The West Asia conflict pushed spot LNG prices to extreme highs ($17–$25/MMBtu), worsening the situation. New government rules, using the Essential Commodities Act to secure gas for essential needs, add to uncertainty and risk for the energy sector, especially gas-fired power plants.

item.title: ACME Solar Adds 33 MW to Rajasthan BESS Project, Eyes 2026 Operations
item.description: ACME Solar has completed the second phase of its Rajasthan Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project, adding 33 MW and 160 MWh of capacity. The system is expected to begin full operations on March 14, 2026. This step brings the company closer to its 300 MW project target, strengthening India's renewable energy grid.

item.title: Circle's Stablecoin Strength Fuels Stock Surge, Outpacing Crypto Peers
item.description: Circle (CRCL) is outperforming crypto-linked equities, with shares up approximately 126% from a February low to trade near $114.20 on March 12, 2026. Analysts at William Blair attribute this strength to USDC's market cap resilience and Circle's dominant stablecoin infrastructure, viewing it as a key layer for global payments. This contrasts with crypto peers, which have largely tracked digital asset downturns. Despite ongoing regulatory uncertainties and competitive pressures, Circle's revenue and on-chain volume show robust growth, positioning it as a significant player in the evolving financial landscape.

item.title: NCLT Approves Accel Limited's Merger with Accel Media Venture
item.description: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), Chennai Bench, approved the merger of Accel Limited and Accel Media Venture Limited. Effective April 1, 2024, the plan combines resources for operational synergies, dissolving Accel Media Venture. Noted items include tax arrears and a director's past SEBI penalty.

item.title: Thyssenkrupp Steel Deal Stalls Over Capital Demands, Restructuring Costs
item.description: Negotiations for Thyssenkrupp's steel division sale to Jindal Steel International are faltering, primarily due to Thyssenkrupp's substantial capital injection demands and the uncertain timing of German state aid. The steel unit faces significant restructuring costs and ongoing losses, overshadowing potential deal progress. Thyssenkrupp shares declined 4.6% on Thursday, reflecting investor concerns about the divestment's complexity. Jindal, meanwhile, has demonstrated investment capacity in European steel acquisitions.

item.title: JK Lakshmi Cement locks Assam limestone; Q3 profit drops 23%
item.description: JK Lakshmi Cement has secured a vital limestone reserve in Assam, supporting its major expansion plans. The company is investing ₹3,000 crore to boost production capacity by March 2028. However, this growth drive is tempered by a 23% drop in third-quarter net profit to ₹57 crore, despite revenue growth, highlighting ongoing margin challenges.

item.title: Avalanche Targets Enterprise: Rebrands 'L1s' to Challenge Rivals
item.description: Avalanche is shifting focus from broad crypto narratives to business-focused blockchain solutions, rebranding its 'subnets' as 'Avalanche L1s' for enterprises. With over 70 live L1s, the network aims to drive revenue growth and cost reduction. This strategic move puts it in direct competition with Ethereum, Solana, and Polygon. Despite the push, AVAX faces market headwinds and significant volatility, trading far below its all-time high.

item.title: Lido Launches Stablecoin Yield Product to Capture DeFi Market
item.description: Lido, the dominant liquid staking provider on Ethereum, has launched Lido Earn, featuring a new EarnUSD vault for stablecoins like USDC and USDT. This move diversifies its offerings, allowing users to earn yield on dollar-pegged assets through automated DeFi strategies. The expansion targets the significant and growing stablecoin market within Ethereum's decentralized finance ecosystem, aiming to simplify access to returns and capture broader market share. Lido is making this strategic move while navigating intense competition and ongoing legal scrutiny surrounding DAO governance.

item.title: NHIT Board to Finalize January Results, Declare Unitholder Payouts March 16
item.description: National Highways Infra Trust's (NHIT) investment manager will hold a board meeting on March 16, 2026. The agenda includes approving unaudited financial statements for the period ending January 31, 2026, and deciding on unitholder distributions for January 2026. This meeting is crucial for unitholders awaiting clarity on performance and payouts.

item.title: India Funds 6G Race with ₹271 Cr, Aims for Global Lead by 2030
item.description: India is backing 104 6G research projects with ₹271 crore through its Telecom Technology Development Fund (TTDF). The move supports the Bharat 6G Vision Document's goal for global leadership by 2030, aiming to boost local innovation and startups. However, the path to commercial success is steep, marked by global competition, the need for startup funding, and tough technical obstacles in building future wireless networks.

item.title: India's Paper Mills Face Cost Squeeze and Export Setback
item.description: India's paper sector is navigating a dual crisis: soaring energy and chemical costs driven by West Asian instability, and a threat from cheaper imports. A weaker rupee adds to production expenses, impacting crucial export markets and domestic profitability. Analysts expect near-term challenges but anticipate margin recovery by FY26.

item.title: Kalpataru Bags ₹1,400 Crore Mumbai Redevelopment Project
item.description: Kalpataru Limited announced a new redevelopment project in Mumbai's Andheri West, expected to generate ₹1,400 crore in Gross Development Value (GDV). The project covers about 3 acres and will add 0.4 million square feet of carpet area. This move highlights Kalpataru's focus on valuable redevelopment projects in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region and strengthens its development pipeline.

item.title: SEBI Plans Smoother Investor Estate Transfers, Doubles Limits
item.description: India's securities regulator, SEBI, is updating rules for transferring assets after an investor's death. The proposed changes aim to lighten the load for grieving families by doubling limits for simplified paperwork and adding a fast-track process for smaller claims. These steps are part of an effort to modernize market operations and improve the investor experience in India's expanding capital markets.

item.title: India Weighs Fuel Exports as Oil Prices Hit $100 Amid Crisis
item.description: India is reviewing requests to send diesel and refined fuels to Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and Maldives amid the escalating West Asia crisis. The Ministry of External Affairs confirmed the review, which balances potential exports with India's own fuel needs and large refining capacity. Meanwhile, India's Foreign Minister is discussing maritime safety and energy security with Iran, as oil prices surge above $100 per barrel due to disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: WeWork India Gains CRISIL Upgrade, Stock Dips as Market Assesses Risks
item.description: WeWork India Management Limited earned strong A+/Stable and A1 ratings from CRISIL for its market position and financial health, with forecasts for 15-20% income growth. Yet, its stock dipped 0.53% on March 12, 2026, as investors weigh the credit upgrade against competitive pressures.

item.title: Ambuja Cements Completes Sanghi Industries Merger Under Adani Group
item.description: Ambuja Cements has officially completed its merger with Sanghi Industries Limited, effective March 12, 2026. This dissolution of Sanghi Industries means its shareholders will receive Ambuja Cements shares based on an approved exchange ratio. The integration strengthens the Adani Group's cement business, boosting operational scale and market reach in India.

item.title: US Jobs: Steady Claims Hide Growing Weakness, Layoffs
item.description: Initial jobless claims for the week ending March 7 stayed at 213,000, slightly below the 215,000 analysts expected. This continues a trend of low claims. However, this surface stability hides underlying labor market issues. February saw an unexpected loss of 92,000 jobs, unemployment rose to 4.4%, and major companies like Morgan Stanley and Block announced significant layoffs. Geopolitical instability, tariff uncertainty, and the Federal Reserve's cautious interest rate policy also create an uncertain economic picture.

item.title: Oil Prices Jump, Stocks Plunge on Mideast Tensions
item.description: Major US stock indexes opened lower as oil prices neared $100 (Brent) and $95 (WTI) amid Middle East conflict. Iran's threats to close the Strait of Hormuz and Iraq's port shutdowns raised supply concerns. While initial jobless claims remained steady at 213,000, focus is now on upcoming PCE inflation data and its effect on Federal Reserve policy.

item.title: Axis Solutions Secures ₹20 Cr HSBC Loan for Expansion, Faces Security Deadline
item.description: Axis Solutions Limited has secured a ₹20 crore working capital loan from HSBC India to fund business expansion. While backed by personal guarantees and company assets, a strict 180-day deadline for finalizing security presents an execution hurdle.

item.title: Brookfield India REIT Plans ₹4,000 Crore Raise for Debt, Growth
item.description: Brookfield India REIT is set to raise ₹4,000 crore via a Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP). The capital infusion targets debt reduction and funding future growth initiatives, bolstering the REIT's financial position post its substantial Ecoworld acquisition. Concurrently, its subsidiary, Ecoworld SPV, will secure an additional ₹1,125 crore from 360 ONE WAM. This strategic move aims to optimize the REIT's Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio and enhance Net Distributable Cash Flow (NDCF).

item.title: Bitcoin Holds Near $70K as Whales Buy During Retail Sell-Off
item.description: Bitcoin is trading near $70,523 even as retail investors sell heavily due to geopolitical tensions. Data shows large 'whale' wallets are buying up Bitcoin, absorbing this selling. Despite market pressures, this demand keeps prices steady. New regulatory cooperation between U.S. agencies and varied analyst price targets for 2026 add to market interest.

item.title: Silver ETFs See First Outflows in 28 Months, But Interest Holds
item.description: February marked a significant shift for silver exchange-traded funds (ETFs) as they recorded their first net outflows in 28 months, totaling Rs 826 crore. This reversal follows a period of record inflows and a sharp 20.2% price correction in the precious metal. Despite the short-term pullback, market experts highlight sustained investor interest, evidenced by growing folio numbers and the ongoing strength of industrial demand, suggesting the outflows are a temporary market reaction rather than a loss of long-term conviction.

item.title: SecUR Credentials Hits ₹2.46 Cr FY24 Loss on Audit, Revenue Falls 44%
item.description: SecUR Credentials posted a net loss of ₹2.46 crore in FY24, a sharp reversal from last year's profit, as revenue dropped 44% to ₹28.13 crore. The company appointed M/s. JPMD & Associates as new auditors and Mr. Bhimsen Vishwanath Pawar as a new director. The auditor's report highlights significant concerns about documentation, compliance, and unverified balances, worsening existing regulatory problems.

item.title: Andhra Pradesh Targets Capgemini for 20,000 Jobs, Pushing Quantum Tech
item.description: Andhra Pradesh is actively seeking French tech giant Capgemini to establish an IT development center and Global Capability Centre (GCC) in Visakhapatnam. The initiative aims to create around 20,000 jobs and foster growth in emerging technologies like AI and quantum computing, supporting Visakhapatnam's ambition as a major IT and data center hub.

item.title: CESC Boosts Renewables With 4 New Subsidiary Companies
item.description: CESC Limited's subsidiary, Purvah Green Power, launched four new wholly-owned entities on March 12, 2026. Each started with ₹1 lakh capital to spearhead expansion into the renewable power sector.

item.title: ADAG Group: ED Seizes ₹581 Cr Assets, CBI Files ₹1085 Cr Fraud Case
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has intensified its crackdown on Anil Ambani's Reliance Group, attaching assets exceeding ₹581 crore across multiple states for Reliance Home Finance and Reliance Commercial Finance. This action is part of an investigation into alleged fund diversion, with cumulative ED attachments now reaching ₹16,310 crore. The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has also filed a fresh case against Anil Ambani and Reliance Communications for alleged bank fraud exceeding ₹1,085 crore.

item.title: Steelco Gujarat Adds Collateral to NCDs, Defers Rights Issue
item.description: Steelco Gujarat Limited's Board of Directors approved changes to its Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs), shifting interest payments to the first day of each fiscal quarter. The company also created collateral security over properties owned by Eesan Estate Developers and Sanish Developers for debenture holders. Discussions regarding a Rights Issue were postponed.

item.title: SecUR Credentials AGM April 3: Board Picks Amid SEBI Scrutiny
item.description: SecUR Credentials Limited will hold its 23rd AGM on April 3, 2026, to approve FY24 financials and key board/auditor appointments. The meeting occurs amid significant SEBI regulatory actions against the company and its former MD, raising shareholder governance concerns.

item.title: Kalpataru Subsidiary's ₹90 Cr Guarantee Adds to Parent's Debt Worries
item.description: Kalpataru's unit KRVPL has guaranteed a ₹90 crore loan for group firm AREDPL. This adds a contingent liability for KRVPL, raising concerns over Kalpataru Limited's existing high debt.

item.title: Eightco Holdings Jumps on $125M Funding for AI & Crypto Pivot
item.description: Eightco Holdings (ORBS) secured $125 million in new institutional funding from major investors like Bitmine Immersion Technologies, ARK Invest, and Kraken. The company also announced it has invested $75 million in key AI and crypto projects, including OpenAI and Worldcoin (WLD). This sharp shift moves ORBS away from its packaging and logistics business into the areas of advanced technology and digital identity.

item.title: Steelco Gujarat Adjusts NCD Payments, Adds Collateral, Defers Rights Issue
item.description: Steelco Gujarat Limited's board approved changes to its Non-Convertible Debenture (NCD) interest payment terms and created collateral for debenture holders. The decision on a potential Rights Issue was postponed. These moves aim to restructure debt and secure investor interests amidst ongoing financial adjustments.

item.title: India's Skills Gap Risks Economic Goals: 47% of Graduates Unemployable
item.description: India needs 109 million skilled workers by 2030, but 47% of graduates are unemployable due to poor critical thinking and digital skills. Shivika Goenka notes the main issue is outdated teaching methods, not a lack of talent. With the world shifting to AI and adaptable skills, India's theory-focused education risks a major gap, threatening its manufacturing and digital ambitions and leaving it less competitive.

item.title: Nikhil Kamath Pushes India's Voice AI & Hardware for Digital Independence
item.description: Nikhil Kamath is backing India's ambition for sovereign voice AI and hardware to cut reliance on foreign tech giants. With global voice AI markets booming and AI hardware advancing, India is at a crucial point. Developing its own technology is key for digital inclusion, economic growth, and national security, favoring open, multilingual tools over dependence on dominant global platforms.

item.title: Lemonn Launches Algo Trading for Retail Investors, Faces SEBI Scrutiny
item.description: Lemonn has launched SmartInvest, offering Indian retail investors automated investment strategies without coding. The platform provides expert-designed, pre-built trading strategies to encourage disciplined investing. It operates under India's new algorithmic trading rules, entering a competitive market targeting data-savvy investors. This comes as over 90% of retail derivative traders face losses.

item.title: ED Seizes Rs 581 Cr More, Reliance Group Assets Hit Rs 16,310 Cr
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) seized Rs 581.65 crore more in assets from Reliance Home Finance (RHFL) and Reliance Commercial Finance (RCFL). This brings the total value of attached assets for the Reliance Anil Ambani Group to over Rs 16,310 crore. The investigation, prompted by Yes Bank, Union Bank of India, and Bank of Maharashtra, focuses on public funds that allegedly became non-performing assets (NPAs) totaling more than Rs 11,000 crore.

item.title: Diligent Media CEO Chitresh Sehgal Steps Down After Less Than a Year
item.description: Diligent Media Corporation announced that CEO Chitresh Sehgal will resign on March 12, 2026. His exit comes less than a year after he was appointed, fueling concerns about leadership stability at the struggling media firm.

item.title: SEBI to Double Securities Transfer Limits, Easing Investor Claims
item.description: India's Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) is set to simplify securities transmission processes. A recent consultation paper proposes significantly higher documentation thresholds: ₹10 lakh for physical securities and ₹30 lakh for dematerialized holdings. These changes aim to ease asset claims for legal heirs and nominees. Additionally, a straight-through processing mechanism for low-value claims is planned, accelerating settlements for smaller estates and reinforcing the market's shift towards digital asset management.

item.title: Edelweiss Launches India's First Passive Hybrid Fund
item.description: Edelweiss Mutual Fund has launched India's first passive hybrid fund following new rules from Sebi. This creates a new option in the growing hybrid fund market, offering investors lower fees and more transparency than active funds.
